summary:
Delays exceeding 48 hours in transferring older adults with proximal femoral fractures to rehab units substantially increase mortality risks—up to five times higher—and impair functional recovery through mechanisms like muscle deconditioning and delirium. These adverse effects underscore the critical importance of early rehabilitation initiation post-fracture.  

\boxed{A}

\boxed{A}. Answer choice: A
